data.body: D5 resolved (thin stdlib helper in infra/autolander). Rationale: the no-import rule is THIS epic's load-bearing decision (keep rebar core platform-agnostic; defer the Gerrit/GitHub/GitLab provider-seam to idea 1701). Peers (Zuul drivers, LUCI/CV vendoring go-gerrit) extract-and-share eventually, which == the deferred seam; but the existing src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (stdlib-only, no rebase/submit surface) is not a drop-in, and a production review_bot refactor mid-feature is out-of-scope risk. So a thin stdlib helper mirroring the proven urllib/Basic-auth/XSSI pattern is the low-risk fit; unification behind the provider-seam happens when 1701 is built. Reverses cleanly to a shared-module extraction if desired. All 7 design decisions now resolved + pygerrit2 premise removed + /rebase:chain rejected as invalid (verified against the live instance's own docs). Re-running plan review.
